commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the remaining blockers include security/API contract decisions around exec delivery and scheduled-turn authority, not only mechanical code repairs.; Needs attention: the PR adds config-triggered exec delivery and broadens scheduled-turn authority without explicit security/operator acceptance.; Review finding: [P1] Add relay to the manifest config schema — extensions/webhooks/sr... (sha=f0f4b0ebfda8018b83c0a190030bf434cf0fd4be)
commands.review_summary:
This PR expands the bundled Webhooks plugin with generic authenticated routes, agent/TaskFlow/channel/exec dispatch, completion delivery, WebSocket relay ingress, docs, tests, and scheduled-turn host-hook changes.

PR surface: Source +2762, Tests +2641, Docs +1172, Config +1, Other +3. Total +6579 across 31 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug report, but the blocking PR findings are source-reproducible: manifest validation rejects the new relay key, and completion context is keyed by session before scheduler acceptance. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the remaining blockers include security/API contract decisions around exec delivery and scheduled-turn authority, not only mechanical code repairs.

target.title: Add generic webhooks plugin
target.body:
## Summary

- add a configurable `webhooks` plugin for authenticated inbound webhook routes
- support route-specific auth, event allowlists, idempotency, fast ACK, TaskFlow dispatch, scheduled agent dispatch, delivery-only routes, and completion delivery
- split the webhook ingress implementation into focused modules for auth, event extraction, idempotency, templating, dispatch, delivery, TaskFlow action compatibility, completion delivery, target wiring, and relay connectivity
- add a WebSocket relay connector so local OpenClaw can receive webhook envelopes over an outbound WebSocket instead of exposing an inbound port
- allow agent completion delivery to channel/log/exec targets so enterprise code review systems can write results back after a real agent run
- add focused tests for auth failures, event filtering, duplicate deliveries, same-path route isolation, templating, scheduled agent dispatch, completion delivery, and relay message handling

## Verification

- `PATH=/Users/bytedance/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:$PATH node scripts/test-extension.mjs webhooks`
- `PATH=/Users/bytedance/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:$PATH pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-misc.config.ts extensions/webhooks`
- `git diff --check`
- real Codebase E2E evidence from the earlier HTTP/tunnel path remains valid for direct ingress: Codebase webhook delivered to local OpenClaw, OpenClaw returned `202`, scheduled the configured agent, ran the review workflow, and wrote an automatic MR review back through the configured exec delivery

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Enterprise systems can post authenticated webhook events into OpenClaw and trigger TaskFlows, scheduled agent runs, or delivery actions without hard-coding a specific provider. This patch also adds the local outbound WebSocket connector needed for relay-backed delivery.

Real environment tested for direct HTTP ingress: Local OpenClaw gateway with the webhooks plugin installed, real Codebase webhook delivery, and a real Codebase merge request in `iaasng/openclaw-session-search`.

Exact steps previously run for the Codebase direct-ingress proof: Configured a Codebase `merge_request` webhook to `/plugins/webhooks/codebase-mr-review`, pushed marker commits to MR `!9`, observed `202 Accepted` deliveries, watched OpenClaw schedule and run the webhook reviewer agent, and confirmed the MR received an automatic review note.

Evidence from the direct-ingress proof: Codebase delivery `776811841624662` reached OpenClaw; logs showed `[webhooks] agent dispatch scheduled`, `[webhooks] exec delivery completed`, and `[webhooks] delivered agent completion`; MR `!9` received automatic review note `776811996803384`.

Observed result from the direct-ingress proof: The webhook request returned quickly, duplicate/event-filter paths remained guarded, the agent reviewed the real MR diff, and the completion delivery wrote the review back to Codebase.

WebSocket relay proof added in this patch: Unit-level relay coverage now verifies that a WebSocket envelope with Codebase-style headers is authenticated through the same route-specific auth path, goes through the same allowlist/idempotency logic, schedules the real agent dispatch path, and returns a relay ack. Duplicate relay deliveries are deduplicated before side effects.

Not yet completed: Real WebSocket relay-backed E2E for GitHub PR, Codebase MR, and Meego requirement-created events. This PR now contains the connector code required on the local OpenClaw side, but the stricter acceptance bar still needs a reachable relay service plus three real platform delivery reports before this PR should be treated as fully production-validated for the relay scenario.
target.author: marchpure
